reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own answer (10 × 9 = 90) is certain, and in all prior games these basic arithmetic puzzles have been almost always correct across all players—only one observed failure in many rounds. That makes the chance that any of Q2–Q4 is wrong much lower than the fixed 25% death risk I face this round if nobody volunteers (and historically B, C, and D don’t volunteer first). Verifying another question this round would still leave me exposed to that same random-death risk and also cost 1 coin, so it doesn’t improve my survival odds. Volunteering immediately with the current capsules maximizes my chance of living and lets me keep all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: I know my own answer for Q2 is correct, and across all previous games A has always volunteered in Round 1 and entered a correct password using the base capsules, so random-death has never actually triggered. Verifying now would just cost 1 coin without changing my survival odds: if A volunteers again, we escape whether I verify or not; if, unexpectedly, everyone passes and a random player dies, my chance of being chosen is the same whether I verified or not. To maximize expected coins with no real loss in safety, I’ll conserve coins and pass.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING:  
Volunteering in Round 1 would mean staking my life on three completely unverified answers, including D’s Q4, and there’s no personal upside when someone else is very likely to step up as in the last several games (B verifies Q1, A immediately volunteers, everyone escapes).  

From a self‑interested view, verifying now is also dominated by waiting:
- If A volunteers again this round and is correct, any verification I do now just burns 1 coin for no benefit.
- If, unexpectedly, nobody volunteers and we reach Round 2, my random‑death risk is the same whether I verified or not, but I’d have already lost 1 coin. I can still choose to verify later if needed.  
- I already know my own answer (Q3: 87−39) is correct, so verifying Q3 is useless.  

Therefore I keep all 4 coins, avoid the volunteer’s death risk, and wait to see if others follow the established pattern this round.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
